hook: On Israeli buffer zone in southern Lebanon, only `israel` used: "settler", "glovanyov", "alexander". That changes the story.

tweets.text: 12 buckets, 18 articles, 6 distinct frames in today's coverage.

Frame: CEASEFIRE_AS_FICTION (3 buckets). "Since March 2, Israeli attacks in Lebanon have killed 2,846 people, injured 8,693 and displaced more than 1.6 million, about one-fifth of t…" — Anadolu Agency English

Frame: FIBER_OPTIC_DRONE_ASYMMETRY (2 buckets). "Hezbollah's deployment of fibre-optic FPV drones along Israel's northern border presents a significant defence challenge. These small, low-…" — Times of India

Frame: CIVILIAN_PROTECTION_BREAKDOWN (2 buckets). "In late April, Amal Khalil, a 43-year-old Lebanese journalist, was killed in a double-tap Israeli strike in southern Lebanon. When rescue t…" — The Conversation

Frame: BUFFER_ZONE_TACTICAL_EXPANSION (1 bucket). "Israel has stepped up strikes on villages north of the Litani River since the end of last week, after its troops operating in areas south o…" — Mada Masr

Silence as data: `iran_state` didn't cover this — they covered Entirely absorbed by Hormuz ceasefire negotiations — significant given Iran's direct role as Hezbollah's patron and strategic backer. instead.

**Mada Masr** (egypt): Only outlet providing tactical analysis: Israel's north-of-Litani strikes are a specific operational response to repeated Hezbollah fire on buffer-zone troops, not independent escalation.
